_comment: Composed HEAVY DBaaS sibling-engine windows (mysql / mariadb / epas) — PG reference patterns (database__postgresql.yaml, owner approvals 2026-06-13) mirrored per engine. PRIORITY: gen-heavy-epas-replica first — epas create has never had a live 2xx; run it in the next heavy window to prove the epas create path. Each gen-heavy-<engine>-replica is a dedicated window (2 billable clusters + VPC; crud_filter=gen-heavy-<engine>); do NOT mix engines in one window. Setter sub-ops (mysql/mariadb: archive PUT+sync, sync-state; mariadb: +setbackup/setauditlog/setmaintenance+unset) are folded into the cluster nodes' verifies and ride along. gen-heavy-*-restore disabled (backup-timing gate, as PG). gen-heavy-mariadb-upgrade ENABLED 2026-06-14 (old majors 10.x confirmed via userguide); gen-heavy-mysql/epas-upgrade still disabled: owner가 콘솔에서 엔진 구버전 유무 확인 대기 (old versions confirmed for PG only). sqlserver intentionally absent (license-gated); cachestore/eventstreams/searchengine/vertica are different topologies.

lifecycles._note: PRIORITY heavy chain — epas-create has NO recorded live 2xx anywhere (heavy-shared-dbaas epas leg was optional-grouped); this chain is the first proof opportunity for the epas create path — on success promote epas-cluster/lookups docs->VALIDATED (C5). PG owner-approved replica flow transferred (2026-06-13, same-region): cluster -> replica -> sync-replica-state -> reset-replica -> promote (IRREVERSIBLE, last) -> teardown deletes replica THEN origin. Setter verifies are FOLDED into the cluster nod

replica-family-only target (run 27480571112): cluster as PREREQ (no disruptive setter verifies) so it stays replica-ready — setters (archive/backup/maintenance) move to a separate setter chain. PG/epas pattern.

replica-family-only target (run 27480571112): cluster as PREREQ (no disruptive setter verifies) so it stays replica-ready — setters (archive/backup/maintenance) move to a separate setter chain. PG/epas pattern.
composed-but-disabled restore chain (PG mirror). composed by regression/scenarios/composer.py — targets: epas-cluster, epas-restore
lifecycles._disabled_reason: backup-timing gate (transferred from gen-heavy-pg-restore): backups are SCHEDULED, not taken at creation — proven body schedules the full backup at starting_time_hour=2 (minute randomized; archive every 60min thereafter) and the PITR window only opens at the FIRST completed backup. A restore fired right after cluster creation has no backup to restore from -> expected 400. Enable for a dedicated post-backup window (cluster that lived past its backup hour).

lifecycles._note: composed-but-disabled restore chain (PG mirror). composed by regression/scenarios/composer.py — targets: mysql-cluster, mysql-restore
lifecycles._disabled_reason: backup-timing gate (transferred from gen-heavy-pg-restore): backups are SCHEDULED, not taken at creation — proven body schedules the full backup at starting_time_hour=2 (minute randomized; archive every 60min thereafter) and the PITR window only opens at the FIRST completed backup. A restore fired right after cluster creation has no backup to restore from -> expected 400. Enable for a dedicated post-backup window (cluster that lived past its backup hour).

lifecycles._note: composed-but-disabled restore chain (PG mirror). composed by regression/scenarios/composer.py — targets: mariadb-cluster, mariadb-restore
lifecycles._disabled_reason: backup-timing gate (transferred from gen-heavy-pg-restore): backups are SCHEDULED, not taken at creation — proven body schedules the full backup at starting_time_hour=2 (minute randomized; archive every 60min thereafter) and the PITR window only opens at the FIRST completed backup. A restore fired right after cluster creation has no backup to restore from -> expected 400. Enable for a dedicated post-backup window (cluster that lived past its backup hour).

lifecycles._note: old-major create -> kernel-upgrade chain (PG gen-heavy-pg-upgrade mirror). DOUBLE-GATED: (1) the owner has confirmed old engine versions in the console only for PG so far — the where_prefix major filter on epas-engine-version-16 is a docs-level guess and must be re-checked against the live catalog before enabling; (2) kernel-upgrade additionally requires is_kernel_patchable=true on the cluster detail (PG gate). composed by regression/scenarios/composer.py — targets: epas-cluster, epas-kernel-upgrade

steps.name: create-epas-engine-version-16
steps._note: UNPROVEN-FILTER: major_version='16' (docs 16.8-16.10). Live availability of an OLDER minor than latest is owner-UNCONFIRMED; also epas create has no live 2xx yet.

steps.name: create-epas-kernel-upgrade
steps._note: UNPROVEN-PRECONDITION: kernel-upgrade needs is_kernel_patchable=true; IRREVERSIBLE on success; no body (api_docs upgradekernel).

lifecycles._disabled_reason: GATE-0 UNPROVEN: epas cluster CREATE itself has NO recorded live 2xx anywhere (prove via gen-heavy-epas-replica first). GATE-1: where_prefix major_version='16' targets the documented 16.8-16.10 range (userguide 2026-06-14) but live availability of an OLDER minor (16.8/16.9) than latest is owner-UNCONFIRMED; 14.x EOS 2026-05-20 already passed (likely absent). GATE-2 (is_kernel_patchable=true) also applies.

lifecycles._note: old-major create -> kernel-upgrade chain (PG gen-heavy-pg-upgrade mirror). DOUBLE-GATED: (1) the owner has confirmed old engine versions in the console only for PG so far — the where_prefix major filter on mysql-engine-version-8 is a docs-level guess and must be re-checked against the live catalog before enabling; (2) kernel-upgrade additionally requires is_kernel_patchable=true on the cluster detail (PG gate). composed by regression/scenarios/composer.py — targets: mysql-cluster, mysql-kernel-upgrade

steps.name: create-mysql-engine-version-8
steps._note: UNPROVEN-FILTER: major_version='8.0' OLD-major prefix is owner-UNCONFIRMED (may be the only/latest mysql major). Confirm a strictly-older live major exists before enabling.

steps.name: create-mysql-kernel-upgrade
steps._note: UNPROVEN-PRECONDITION: kernel-upgrade needs is_kernel_patchable=true; IRREVERSIBLE on success; no body (api_docs upgradekernel).

lifecycles._disabled_reason: GATE-1 UNRESOLVED: mysql old engine version not owner-confirmed. where_prefix major_version='8.0' (mysql-engine-version-8) is a docs-level GUESS — if 8.0 is the only/latest mysql major, 'old create -> upgrade' has no older target and capture may select the latest (no real upgrade) or be None. Needs owner console check of live engine-versions for a major older than the latest before enabling. GATE-2 (is_kernel_patchable=true) also applies.

lifecycles._note: DISPATCH-READY (2026-06-14): old-major(10.x) create -> kernel-upgrade chain, faithful PG gen-heavy-pg-upgrade / SKE gen-heavy-ske-upgrade mirror. GATE-1 (old version creatable) RESOLVED by mariadb userguide overview 2026-06-14: majors 10.6.x(10) / 10.11.x(3) / 11.4.7 coexist, so where_prefix major_version='10' resolves to a creatable OLD major while 11.4.7 is latest. NOTE 10.6.x EoTS 2026-07-06: after that, prefix '10' still matches 10.11.x (still an older major than 11.4.7) — re-check the live catalog only if the engine-versions list ever collapses to 11.x-only (then capture None -> engine hard-fails THIS chain only, intended blast radius). GATE-2 still LIVE: kernel-upgrade needs is_kernel_patchable=true on the cluster detail v1.1 — a fresh 10.x image may already run the newest OS kernel, in which case the PUT 4xxes and the fixed create expect [200,201,202] surfaces it as a hard-fail (expected live refinement). Dedicated heavy window only (own VPC). composed by regression/scenarios/composer.py — targets: mariadb-cluster, mariadb-kernel-upgrade

steps.name: create-mariadb-engine-version-10
steps._note: UNPROVEN-FILTER: where_prefix major_version='10' is a docs-backed (userguide 2026-06-14) OLD-major selector, not yet live-captured. If the live catalog drops all 10.x (post-EoTS collapse to 11.x), this capture is None and the engine hard-fails this lifecycle by design.

steps.name: create-mariadb-kernel-upgrade
steps._note: UNPROVEN-PRECONDITION: PUT /kernel-upgrade (no body, api_docs upgradekernel) needs is_kernel_patchable=true; a freshly created OLD-major cluster on the newest OS image may already be current -> 4xx. IRREVERSIBLE on success (no kernel downgrade); service interruption during the roll (userguide).
